channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Veteran South African actress Cynthia Shange passes away at 76 after illness
item.description: South African actress Cynthia Shange, widely recognised for her role in the popular soapie Muvhango, has passed away at the age of 76. She died in a hospital in KwaZulu-Natal after being ill for some ...

item.title: Tim Cook to step down as Apple CEO and transition to executive chairman, with John Ternus appointed successor
item.description: Apple has announced a major leadership transition, with long-serving Chief Executive Officer Tim Cook stepping down from his role after nearly 15 years at the helm. Cook will not leave the company ent...

item.title: Tanzania urges coordinated African action on water security and sustainable resource management
item.description: Tanzania’s Minister for Finance, Mr Khamis Mussa Omar, has called on African nations to strengthen strategic planning and cooperation in order to secure reliable access to safe and clean water for bot...

item.title: Kenya Power schedules maintenance-related power outages in nine counties on April 21
item.description: Kenya Power and Lighting Company (KPLC) has announced planned electricity interruptions affecting several parts of nine counties on Tuesday, April 21. The utility firm explained that the temporary out...

item.title: How Iran’s AI-generated meme campaigns are shaping online propaganda and reaching global audiences
item.description: Iran-linked media groups are increasingly using AI-generated memes and videos as part of a modern propaganda strategy that spreads widely across social media platforms. One of the most prominent actor...

item.title: Veteran South African actress and former Miss World finalist Cynthia Shange dies at 76
item.description: Veteran South African actress and former beauty queen Cynthia Shange has died at the age of 76 after an illness. She passed away in the early hours of Monday at a hospital in KwaZulu-Natal. Her death ...

item.title: Pakistan steps up security and diplomatic efforts ahead of possible second US-Iran talks in Islamabad
item.description: Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi held separate meetings with the Iranian and US envoys in Islamabad to review preparations for a possible second round of talks between Washington and Tehran. These effor...

item.title: Court orders immediate release of detained Zaria cleric, awards damages for rights violation
item.description: Federal High Court for Abuja don order make dem release Sheikh Sani Abdulkadir Zaria immediately, after the judge rule say security agencies and one commercial bank violate him fundamental human right...

item.title: Zoologist and author Desmond Morris passes away at 98
item.description: Desmond Morris was a widely known British zoologist, author, surrealist painter, and television presenter whose work brought animal behavior and human evolutionary biology to a broad popular audience....

item.title: PCB bans Dasun Shanaka for one PSL season after contract breach to join IPL
item.description: The Pakistan Cricket Board (PCB) has imposed a one-year ban on Sri Lankan all-rounder Dasun Shanaka after he withdrew from the 2026 Pakistan Super League (PSL) to participate in the Indian Premier Lea...

item.title: Search continues for two missing crew after MV Jozani II sinks off Tanzanian coast
item.description: Search operations for two missing crew members from the MV Jozani II have now entered the third day, with Tanzanian maritime authorities continuing efforts despite difficult sea conditions. The vessel...

item.title: Doug Ford responds to private jet controversy and broader political scrutiny in Canada
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford has responded to criticism over his government’s decision to purchase a pre-owned $28.9-million Bombardier Challenger 650 private jet, saying he reversed the decision after h...

item.title: Villar family rejects SEC allegations of insider trading and market manipulation tied to Villar Land Holdings
item.description: The Villar family, including billionaire businessman Manny Villar and several of his relatives, has formally denied allegations of insider trading, stock market manipulation, and other violations of t...

item.title: Tanzania Secures Hosting Rights for Miss World 2027, Boosting Tourism and Creative Industries
item.description: Tanzania has officially secured the rights to host the 74th Miss World pageant in 2027 after signing a formal agreement in Dar es Salaam. The announcement was made during a ceremony attended by govern...

item.title: The making of ‘What a Beautiful Day’ by the Levellers and its unexpected radio ban following Princess Diana’s death
item.description: The Levellers’ song What a Beautiful Day, written by vocalist and guitarist Mark Chadwick, emerged in late 1996 during a period of political transition in the UK. Chadwick describes the track as layer...

item.title: KNCHR invites public input on Draft Reparations Guidelines 2026 following court ruling and policy reforms
item.description: The Kenya National Commission on Human Rights (KNCHR) has opened a public participation exercise inviting members of the public and stakeholders to give feedback on the Draft Reparations Guidelines 20...

item.title: WestJet reduces June seat capacity amid rising jet fuel prices
item.description: WestJet Airlines is reducing its flight capacity for June as it responds to sustained increases in jet fuel prices driven by global supply disruptions. The Calgary-based carrier announced it will cut ...

item.title: TTAP rejects reports of rift with Imran Khan’s family, reaffirms strong alliance
item.description: The Tehreek Tahaffuz Aiyeen-e-Pakistan (TTAP) has strongly rejected media reports suggesting any internal differences or rifts with the family of jailed former Prime Minister and PTI founder Imran Kha...

item.title: OWWA Seeks P12 Billion to Support Repatriation and Reintegration of OFWs Amid Middle East Crisis
item.description: The Overseas Workers Welfare Administration (OWWA) is requesting a P12-billion supplemental budget to support the repatriation and reintegration of overseas Filipino workers (OFWs) affected by the ong...

item.title: KIM Reviews TVET Closure Order Amid Student Concerns
item.description: The Kenya Institute of Management (KIM) has dismissed a closure notice issued by the Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority (TVETA) on April 20, 2026, which revoked the institution'...
